areas.name: Longboat Key
areas.description: Longboat Key is a 10-mile barrier island between Sarasota Bay and the Gulf of Mexico — and one of Florida's quietest luxury resort communities. The island is overwhelmingly residential, favoring low-rise condominiums and Gulf-front and bayfront homes over commercial development, and beach access is the main event: miles of soft, uncrowded sand define daily life, kept serene in part by deliberately limited public parking and very little retail. At its heart, The Resort at Longboat Key Club anchors island life with 45 holes of championship golf — the Gulf-side Islandside course and the bayfront Harbourside layouts — alongside the deep-water Longboat Key Club Moorings, a 291-slip marina that berths vessels up to 150 feet. The 2024 arrival of The St. Regis Longboat Key Resort, set on 18 beachfront acres with 800 feet of private Gulf frontage, has further sharpened the island's standing among discerning buyers. The result is a market defined by privacy, water, and a slower, more residential rhythm than anywhere else on the Sarasota coast.

faqs.question: What is the average home price on Longboat Key?
faqs.answer: As of {{asOf}}, the median list price across {{active.count}} active Longboat Key listings is {{active.medianPrice}}, and the median sold price across the most recent closed sales is {{sold.medianPrice}}. Gulf-front properties typically command well above the median, while bay-side condos in the island's vintage buildings remain the most attainable entry point. Data sourced from Stellar MLS via PostGIS-verified geographic boundaries.

faqs.question: Is Longboat Key a good place to invest in real estate?
faqs.answer: Longboat Key has historically been one of the most stable luxury markets on Florida's Gulf Coast. Limited buildable land on the barrier island constrains supply, while demand from affluent buyers seeking waterfront living remains strong. The island's strict building regulations help protect property values long-term.

faqs.question: What are the best neighborhoods on Longboat Key?
faqs.answer: Popular communities include The Resort at Longboat Key Club (golf and beach resort living), Harbourside (bayfront condos with marina access), Bird Key Yacht Club area, and the exclusive estates along Gulf of Mexico Drive. The north end tends to be quieter, while the south end offers easier access to St. Armands Circle.

areas.name: St. Armands and Lido
areas.tagline: Barrier island living — beachfront condos to Circle-side estates
areas.description: The St. Armands & Lido market covers the entire Lido Key barrier island, from the beachfront condos along Benjamin Franklin Drive to the residential streets surrounding St. Armands Circle. The area is connected to mainland Sarasota via the Ringling Causeway, and combines Gulf-front beach living, walkable luxury shopping and dining at St. Armands Circle, and a mix of mid-century condos and new-construction Gulf-front estates.

faqs.question: What is the median price for homes on St. Armands and Lido?
faqs.answer: As of {{asOf}}, the median list price for active homes on St. Armands and Lido is {{active.medianPrice}}, with a median sold price of {{sold.medianPrice}} across the most recent closed sales. Active inventory spans {{active.count}} listings, from entry-level condos to new-construction Gulf-front estates. Data sourced from Stellar MLS via PostGIS-verified geographic boundaries — listings are assigned to St. Armands and Lido based on actual coordinates, not just postal code.

faqs.question: What is the price per square foot on St. Armands and Lido?
faqs.answer: The median price per square foot for active St. Armands and Lido listings is {{active.medianPricePerSqFt}} as of {{asOf}}. Beachfront and direct Gulf-view condos typically command a meaningful premium above the median, while bayside and inland Lido Shores properties trade below it.

Properties priced near the median tend to move faster, while ultra-luxury Gulf-front estates and large condo units typically take longer. Sale-to-list ratios meaningfully below asking, combined with extended marketing times, generally give qualified buyers negotiating leverage — particularly on properties that have been on market more than 60 days.

faqs.question: How many waterfront properties are available on St. Armands and Lido?
faqs.answer: {{active.waterfrontCount}} of {{active.count}} active St. Armands and Lido listings are waterfront properties as of {{asOf}}. Coverage includes Gulf-front beach condos along Benjamin Franklin Drive, bayfront and canal-front estates in Lido Shores, and dock-equipped homes along the inner basin. Geographic verification is performed via PostGIS against actual coastline polygons rather than relying on agent-entered MLS waterfront flags.

faqs.question: What does $1 million buy you on St. Armands and Lido?
faqs.answer: In the $900,000 to $1.5 million range, current St. Armands and Lido inventory includes {{priceBands.band900kTo1_5m}} active listings — typically mid-floor 2-bedroom condos with Gulf or bay views, smaller bayside single-family homes, and select units in established beachfront buildings such as L'Elegance and Key Tower. Below $900k there are {{priceBands.under900k}} active listings, mostly older or interior-facing condo units.

faqs.question: What is the most expensive recent sale on St. Armands and Lido?
faqs.answer: In spring 2026, the most expensive recorded sale on St. Armands and Lido was 171 Whittier Drive in Lido Beach Division A — a 5-bedroom, 6.5-bath new-construction Gulf-front estate built in 2023 with approximately 6,367 square feet of living space, which closed at $15,800,000 in April 2026. Other high-end closings in the same period included luxury condos at L'Elegance on Lido Beach in the $1.8M to $2.5M range.

areas.name: Siesta Key
areas.description: Siesta Key is renowned for its #1-ranked white quartz sand beach and vibrant village atmosphere. The island offers everything from Gulf-front condos to canal-front homes, with a lively dining and nightlife scene in Siesta Key Village.

faqs.question: What is the average home price on Siesta Key?
faqs.answer: As of {{asOf}}, the median list price across {{active.count}} active Siesta Key listings is {{active.medianPrice}}, with a median sold price of {{sold.medianPrice}} across the most recent closed sales. The market ranges widely — from village-adjacent condos to Gulf-front estates exceeding $5 million on the north end of the island. Data sourced from Stellar MLS via PostGIS-verified geographic boundaries.

faqs.question: Why is Siesta Key beach so famous?
faqs.answer: Siesta Key Beach is famous for its 99% pure quartz crystal sand, which stays cool even on the hottest days. It has been ranked the #1 beach in America multiple times by Dr. Beach and TripAdvisor. The wide, flat beach and calm Gulf waters make it ideal for families and visitors year-round.

areas.name: Downtown Sarasota
areas.description: Downtown Sarasota is the Gulf Coast's most concentrated luxury condo market — a high-rise corridor along Sarasota Bay anchored by landmark buildings like The Owen, The Edge, Ritz-Carlton Residences, and AQUA. The area combines bayfront views, walkable access to St. Armands Causeway, the Bayfront Park, the Sarasota Opera House, and a dense restaurant and gallery scene, with active new construction continuing to reshape the skyline. This page covers the urban core excluding the Rosemary District.

faqs.question: What is the median price for a downtown Sarasota condo?
faqs.answer: As of {{asOf}}, the median list price for active downtown Sarasota residences is {{active.medianPrice}}, with a median sold price of {{sold.medianPrice}} across the most recent closed sales. The market spans from entry-level condos to ultra-luxury penthouses in buildings such as AQUA on Golden Gate Point. Data sourced from Stellar MLS via PostGIS-verified geographic boundaries (Rosemary District excluded).

faqs.question: What is the price per square foot in downtown Sarasota?
faqs.answer: The median price per square foot for active downtown Sarasota listings is {{active.medianPricePerSqFt}} as of {{asOf}}. Bayfront and Golden Gate Point penthouses regularly exceed $1,500/sqft, while interior-facing units in older mid-rise buildings trade well below the median.

faqs.question: How many condos are for sale in downtown Sarasota?

The active inventory is concentrated in high-rise towers along the bayfront and Main Street corridor, with the balance in mid-rise and low-rise buildings.

faqs.answer: The median days on market for sold downtown Sarasota condos is approximately {{sold.medianDom}} days as of {{asOf}}. The current sale-to-list ratio is {{sold.saleToListRatio}}, meaning sellers are typically accepting offers below their original asking price — a softness signal that gives qualified buyers room to negotiate, particularly on older inventory.

faqs.question: What is the typical HOA or condo fee in downtown Sarasota?
faqs.answer: The median monthly condo fee for downtown Sarasota properties is approximately {{fees.medianMonthlyCondoFee}}, with median total monthly association costs of {{fees.medianMonthlyAssociation}}, as of {{asOf}}. High-end buildings with concierge service, pools, fitness centers, and bay views (such as AQUA, The Tower Residences, and BLVD Sarasota) trend higher, often $3,000+ per month for larger residences. Older mid-rise buildings carry meaningfully lower fees.

faqs.question: Is downtown Sarasota a buyer's or seller's market in 2026?
faqs.answer: As of {{asOf}}, downtown Sarasota shows a sale-to-list ratio of {{sold.saleToListRatio}}, a median {{sold.medianDom}} days on market, and {{active.count}} active listings against {{sold.count}} recent closed sales. {{active.newConstructionCount}} of the active listings are post-2020 construction, and that volume of new product delivering simultaneously contributes to elevated absorption time — especially in the $1.5M–$3M segment, where {{priceBands.band1_5mTo3m}} active listings compete.

faqs.question: How much new construction is available in downtown Sarasota?
faqs.answer: Approximately {{active.newConstructionCount}} of {{active.count}} active downtown Sarasota listings are new construction built in 2020 or later. The downtown skyline is in an active building cycle, with major projects including The Owen, The Edge, Ritz-Carlton Residences, Epoch, and Rosewood Residences delivering or pre-selling. This is the largest concentration of new luxury condo construction on Florida's Gulf Coast.

faqs.question: What does $1.5 million buy in downtown Sarasota?
faqs.answer: Between $1.5M and $3M, current downtown Sarasota inventory includes {{priceBands.band1_5mTo3m}} active listings — typically larger 2- and 3-bedroom condos with bay views, mid-floor units in landmark buildings, or new-construction residences in pre-completion projects. Below that, {{priceBands.under900k}} active listings sit under $900k and {{priceBands.band900kTo1_5m}} fall in the $900k–$1.5M band. Above $3M there are {{priceBands.band3mPlus}} active listings, the highest concentration being penthouse and bayfront units.

areas.name: West of Trail
areas.tagline: Sarasota's most coveted mainland luxury corridor
areas.description: West of Trail is the legacy luxury mainland district immediately south of downtown Sarasota and west of US-41 (the Tamiami Trail). The core stretches from Mound Street south through neighborhoods like Cherokee Park, Hudson Bayou, McClellan Park, Avondale, and Oyster Bay — tree-lined streets, mid-century estates, and a tight inventory of high-end single-family homes within walking or biking distance of the bayfront and Southside Village.

faqs.question: What is West of Trail in Sarasota?
faqs.answer: West of Trail refers to the mainland luxury district immediately west of US-41 (the Tamiami Trail) and south of downtown Sarasota. The core area includes Cherokee Park, Hudson Bayou, McClellan Park, Avondale, and Oyster Bay — Sarasota's most established residential corridor for legacy single-family luxury homes.

areas.name: West of Trail - North
areas.description: The northern extension of the West of Trail luxury corridor — covering the Indian Beach / Sapphire Shores / Bay Haven neighborhoods stretching from just north of downtown up toward the Ringling Museum and New College campus. Bayfront-adjacent estates, mid-century modern enclaves, and historic waterfront properties.

faqs.question: What is West of Trail North?
faqs.answer: West of Trail North covers the luxury mainland neighborhoods north of downtown Sarasota — including Indian Beach, Sapphire Shores, and Bay Haven — stretching up toward the Ringling Museum and New College. The area is known for bayfront-adjacent estates, mid-century modern architecture, and proximity to Sarasota's arts and cultural anchors.

areas.name: West of Trail - South
areas.description: The southern extension of West of Trail — covering the mainland luxury corridor from the south end of the core area down to Stickney Point Road, including Phillippi Estates, Bay Shore, Country Club Shores-adjacent mainland parcels, and the deep-water canal communities west of US-41 approaching Siesta Key. Waterfront-heavy with substantial single-family inventory.

faqs.question: What is West of Trail South?
faqs.answer: West of Trail South covers the southern mainland luxury corridor from the south end of the West of Trail core area down to Stickney Point Road. The area includes Phillippi Estates, Bay Shore, and the deep-water canal communities approaching the Siesta Key bridges — a waterfront-heavy market with substantial single-family inventory.

areas.name: Bird Key
areas.description: Bird Key is an exclusive man-made island community located between the Ringling Causeway and St. Armands Circle. Known for its waterfront estates, deep-water docks, and private yacht club, Bird Key is one of Sarasota's most prestigious addresses.

faqs.question: How much do homes cost on Bird Key?
faqs.answer: Bird Key is one of Sarasota's most exclusive addresses. As of {{asOf}}, the median list price across {{active.count}} active Bird Key listings is {{active.medianPrice}}, with a median sold price of {{sold.medianPrice}} across recent closed sales. Limited inventory — typically under 30 active listings at any time — keeps values strong.

areas.name: Palmer Ranch
areas.tagline: South Sarasota's master-planned community of gated golf villages
areas.description: Palmer Ranch is the largest master-planned community on the south Sarasota mainland — roughly 7.5 square miles between US-41 and I-75, originally developed in the late 1980s and now home to more than 30 distinct villages and subdivisions. The community is anchored by TPC Prestancia and The Country Club of Sarasota, with a deep bench of gated single-family neighborhoods including Deer Creek, Stoneybrook, Silver Oak, Mira Lago, Prestancia, and newer enclaves like Esplanade on Palmer Ranch, Sandhill Preserve, and Hammock Preserve. Residents have direct access to the 18.5-mile Legacy Trail, the Pine View School attendance zone, and roughly ten-minute drives to both Siesta Key beaches and downtown Sarasota.

faqs.question: What is the median home price in Palmer Ranch?
faqs.answer: As of {{asOf}}, the median list price for active Palmer Ranch homes is {{active.medianPrice}}, with a median sold price of {{sold.medianPrice}} across the most recent closed sales. Active inventory spans entry-level condos to estate homes in Esplanade on Palmer Ranch and the Wildwood/Prestancia corridor. Data sourced from Stellar MLS via PostGIS-verified geographic boundaries — Palmer Ranch listings are assigned based on actual coordinates falling inside the Census-defined Palmer Ranch CDP plus a small northern extension covering Esplanade and Sandhill Preserve, not just postal code.

faqs.question: What is the price per square foot in Palmer Ranch?
faqs.answer: The median price per square foot for active Palmer Ranch listings is {{active.medianPricePerSqFt}} as of {{asOf}}. New-construction homes in Esplanade on Palmer Ranch and Sandhill Preserve generally command a premium above $300/sqft, while villas and condos in 1990s-era communities such as Huntington Pointe, Stoneybrook at Palmer Ranch, and the Prestancia condo buildings typically trade between $200 and $250 per square foot.

faqs.question: How many homes are for sale in Palmer Ranch right now?
faqs.answer: There are currently {{active.count}} active Palmer Ranch listings, with another {{counts.pending}} under contract (pending) and {{sold.count}} closed sales recorded in recent months. Approximately {{active.waterfrontCount}} of the active listings are waterfront — typically lake-front, pond-front, or preserve-adjacent rather than coastal — and {{active.newConstructionCount}} are new construction built in 2020 or later. Inventory skews accessible: {{priceBands.under900k}} of the active listings are priced below $900,000, with only {{priceBands.band1_5mTo3m}} between $1.5M and $3M and {{priceBands.band3mPlus}} above $3M.

faqs.question: How long does it take to sell a home in Palmer Ranch?
faqs.answer: The median days on market for sold Palmer Ranch listings is approximately {{sold.medianDom}} days as of {{asOf}}. The current sale-to-list ratio is {{sold.saleToListRatio}}, meaning sellers are typically accepting offers below their original asking price, but with notably less discount than buyers are seeing on the Sarasota barrier islands in the same period. Properties priced below the area median tend to move faster; ultra-luxury listings above $1.5M can sit 120 days or longer.

faqs.question: Is Palmer Ranch a buyer's or seller's market in 2026?
faqs.answer: Based on a {{sold.saleToListRatio}} sale-to-list ratio, a median {{sold.medianDom}} days on market, and {{active.count}} active listings against {{sold.count}} recent closed sales as of {{asOf}}, Palmer Ranch sits closer to a balanced market than Sarasota's coastal luxury submarkets. The broader buyer pool at sub-$900,000 price points, ongoing relocation demand into south Sarasota County, and the Pine View School attendance draw all keep absorption healthier in Palmer Ranch than in the ultra-luxury barrier-island and downtown segments.

faqs.question: What does $500,000 buy in Palmer Ranch?
faqs.answer: In the $400,000 to $700,000 range, current Palmer Ranch inventory includes 1990s-era single-family homes in Stoneybrook at Palmer Ranch and Deer Creek, maintenance-free villas in Huntington Pointe and Prestancia, and select three-bedroom condominiums. Comparable sales in this range from spring 2026 included 8972 Huntington Pointe Dr (a 1,827 sqft 3-bedroom that closed at $525,000), 4501 Murcia Blvd #7 in Prestancia ($482,500), 4565 Deer Creek Blvd ($650,000), and 4223 Balmoral Way in Stoneybrook at Palmer Ranch ($625,000). Below $400,000, inventory is dominated by condominiums in 1990–2005 era buildings.

faqs.question: How much new construction is available in Palmer Ranch?
faqs.answer: Approximately {{active.newConstructionCount}} of {{active.count}} active Palmer Ranch listings are new construction built in 2020 or later. The newer enclaves driving that supply are Esplanade on Palmer Ranch (resort-style amenities, age-restricted sections), Sandhill Preserve, Hammock Preserve, and Legacy Estates at Esplanade on Palmer Ranch. In mid-2026 the highest-priced active new-construction listings included homes in Legacy Estates at Esplanade on Palmer Ranch approaching $2.3M.

faqs.question: What is Palmer Ranch?
faqs.answer: Palmer Ranch is a 10,000+ acre master-planned community in unincorporated south Sarasota County, Florida, originally developed in the late 1980s by Palmer Ranch Holdings. The community spans from US-41 east to I-75 and contains more than 30 distinct villages and subdivisions, ranging from gated single-family golf-course communities to age-restricted enclaves and newer townhome developments. Palmer Ranch is a U.S. Census Designated Place (CDP) with a residential population of approximately 15,000.

faqs.question: What neighborhoods are in Palmer Ranch?
faqs.answer: Palmer Ranch contains over 30 individual villages. Notable communities include TPC Prestancia and Prestancia (golf-course estates), The Country Club of Sarasota, Deer Creek, Stoneybrook at Palmer Ranch, Silver Oak, Mira Lago, Marbella, Cobblestone on Palmer Ranch, Promenade Estates, and the newer Esplanade on Palmer Ranch, Sandhill Preserve, and Hammock Preserve communities. The mix spans gated single-family estates, maintenance-free villas, and condo/townhome enclaves.

faqs.question: What schools serve Palmer Ranch?
faqs.answer: Palmer Ranch is served by the Sarasota County school district. The community sits in the attendance zone for Ashton Elementary, Sarasota Middle, and Riverview High School. Palmer Ranch is also home to Pine View School — a K-12 public school for gifted students consistently ranked among the top public high schools in Florida and the United States — which draws applications from across the county.

faqs.question: Is Palmer Ranch a gated community?
faqs.answer: Palmer Ranch as a whole is not gated, but the majority of its individual villages are. Communities such as Deer Creek, Stoneybrook at Palmer Ranch, Silver Oak, Prestancia, The Country Club of Sarasota, Esplanade on Palmer Ranch, and Sandhill Preserve all have private entry gates. A handful of older or smaller villages within the master plan are non-gated.

faqs.question: Does Palmer Ranch have golf courses?
faqs.answer: Yes. Palmer Ranch is home to TPC Prestancia (one of only two PGA Tour-recognized clubs in the Sarasota market), which features 36 holes across the Stadium and Club courses, as well as The Country Club of Sarasota with its 18-hole championship course. Stoneybrook at Palmer Ranch also has its own resident-focused 18-hole course. Several other villages are golf-cart-friendly even without on-site golf.

faqs.question: How close is Palmer Ranch to Siesta Key beach?
faqs.answer: Palmer Ranch sits roughly 4 to 6 miles east of the Siesta Key bridges, with most villages within a 10- to 15-minute drive of Siesta Key Beach via Stickney Point Road or Clark Road. Downtown Sarasota is approximately 8 miles north, also a 10- to 15-minute drive depending on traffic.

faqs.question: What is the Legacy Trail and how does Palmer Ranch connect to it?
faqs.answer: The Legacy Trail is an 18.5-mile paved rail-trail running from Payne Park in downtown Sarasota south to Venice, with multiple access points cutting directly through Palmer Ranch. Trail access is a major lifestyle draw for the community — several villages back directly onto the trail, and the broader Palmer Ranch master plan integrates pedestrian and cycling connections to it.
